deployment.name: 3-Agent Customer Support Fleet (Triage → KB-Lookup → Responder)
deployment.description: Three-agent customer-support pattern derived from Decagon / Ada / Sierra public talks and the classic 3-tier support-agent architecture. A cheap fast Triage agent routes incoming queries; a RAG-heavy KB-Lookup agent retrieves grounded evidence; a longer-context Responder agent composes the user-facing reply with inline MiniCheck verification. Two segments (authenticated customers + anonymous visitors) reflect the canonical support-traffic shape: anon spikes drive volume, auth multi-turn drives token cost. Use this preset to size API spend for a production support deployment in the 25K-MAU range.

anchor_query.example: I was charged twice for my October subscription — can you refund the duplicate and confirm my plan?

_calibration.source: Pattern derived from Decagon / Ada / Sierra public talks + classic 3-tier support agent architecture; specific token sizes are estimates
_calibration.reference: Decagon engineering blog; Ada conversational-AI architecture diagrams; Sierra agent OS announcement
_calibration.notes: Token sizes (Triage 1.5K in / 200 out, KB-Lookup 3K in / 800 out, Responder 4.5K in / 1.5K out) are reasonable midpoints for production support fleets. KB-Lookup's 3-5 file_search calls/query is on the higher end and reflects a deployment with non-trivial knowledge-base depth. Responder runs MiniCheck inline (single-call NLI verifier) at 100% coverage on its output.

web_search.label: Web Search
web_search.description: Provider-managed web search — used by Responder for current-event / breaking-issue lookups.

file_search.label: File Search (knowledge base)
file_search.description: Provider-managed RAG over support KB articles. KB-Lookup hits this 3-5× per query.

crm_lookup.label: CRM lookup (MCP)
crm_lookup.description: Custom MCP server — pulls account context (plan, recent invoices, ticket history) for the Triage and KB-Lookup agents.

ticketing_mcp.label: Ticketing system (MCP)
ticketing_mcp.description: Custom MCP server — creates / updates support tickets when the Responder escalates.

agents.label: Triage (intent classifier + router)
agents.description: Cheap, fast intent classifier. Looks at the inbound message + minimal CRM context and routes to the right downstream agent (KB-Lookup, billing-flow, escalate-to-human). Always runs.

agents.label: KB-Lookup (RAG-heavy retrieval)
agents.description: Pulls grounded evidence from the support KB via file_search (3-5×/query) and account state via CRM. Output is a structured evidence packet for the Responder.

agents.label: Responder (user-facing reply + MiniCheck)
agents.description: Composes the user-facing reply from the KB-Lookup evidence packet. MiniCheck (single-call NLI verifier) runs inline on every response (coverage=1.0) to catch grounding regressions before delivery.

escalate.description: Routed to human — Responder writes a brief handoff

default.label: Support mix (50% full · 30% lookup · 12% escalate · 8% refusal)

segments.label: Authenticated customers (signed in)
segments.description: Logged-in paying customers. Multi-turn sessions (account-context heavy), warm cache, no bot inflation. ~2.4 support contacts per customer per month.

segments.label: Anonymous visitors (public help widget)
segments.description: Anonymous spike traffic from the public-facing help widget. Short sessions, cold cache (1.5× bot factor applied). Volume is bursty but each contact is small.
